what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard block item that analyzes the current document model and enumerates the specific missing pieces needed to support hierarchical, type-aware selection.
observations: The document is currently assembled as a flat list: one document item and direct child blocks linked via part_of, read exactly one level deep.
The part_of edge primitive is described as already capable of chaining into a tree, even though current data uses only one level.
It identifies three concrete gaps: a hierarchical parser, a recursive assemble_document, and a file-discovered node-type registry.
It proposes a node-type registry modeled after existing registry patterns like item_types/, board_edges/, and mark_types/.
It is authored by claude_code in the dragnet-development channel and links part_of to board://item-e42d651f.
standouts: The reframing of the gap as 'flat list, not a tree' with a narrower, more precise scope.
The proposal that gesture semantics should live in a file-discovered node-type registry rather than in gesture code.
The final verdict that only three scoped pieces need to be built, not a full document model from scratch.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history fields.
Represents relationships via a links array with kind and target.
Uses markdown body with headings, bold emphasis, and numbered lists.
References code symbols and functions with backticks.
concerns: Assertions about cc_board.py and assemble_document behavior are made without the code being present in this file.
The proposed node-type registry is described by analogy but no schema or row format is specified here.
notes: A design note narrowing the document-tree gap to three build tasks: recursive parser, recursive assembler, and a file-discovered node-type registry.
questions: What is the content and role of the parent item board://item-e42d651f?
What exact schema and fields would the proposed node-type registry contain?
How would type-aware gesture semantics be encoded in registry rows?
What is the intended scope and trigger of 'intelligent selection' being designed for?
purpose_vs_actual: yes — the stated purpose is to analyze the document model versus what intelligent selection needs, and the body does exactly that by listing concrete gaps
apparent_themes: separation of edge primitives from missing tooling
intention_signals: It is trying to show that the hierarchical document model is closer to existing infrastructure than it appears, and to redirect effort toward three concrete, scoped build items plus a registry-based vocabulary for selection semantics.
novelty: It frames the gap as narrower than 'no tree' by distinguishing the existing recursive-capable edge primitive from the missing parser, reader, and typed vocabulary; it also proposes a file-discovered node-type registry as the place where selection semantics live.
contribution: It contributes a scoped decomposition of the document-model gap and a design pattern proposal: a file-discovered node-type registry that would hold type-aware selection semantics.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard design note analyzing the current flat, one-level document assembly model and identifying the missing hierarchical parser, recursive assembler, and file-discovered node-type registry needed for tree-aware intelligent selection.
intention_for_embedding: To refine the document-model gap into three concrete, scoped build items and propose a registry-driven vocabulary for hierarchical selection semantics.
